question_text: Which combination of energy resources would best balance reliability, cost, and environmental impact for a modern UK city?

options.label: A
options.text: 100% coal power because it is reliable and historically cheap

options.label: B
options.text: A mix of nuclear (base load), wind and solar (variable), with some natural gas for backup - balancing reliability with low carbon emissions and manageable costs

options.label: C
options.text: 100% renewables (wind and solar) because they produce zero emissions

options.label: D
options.text: 100% natural gas because it is cleaner than coal and cheaper than renewables

explanation: An energy mix using multiple sources provides reliability (nuclear provides constant baseload, gas provides flexible backup), cost-effectiveness (renewable costs are falling), and low emissions. Pure renewables alone cannot guarantee supply during low wind/sun periods, while fossil fuels alone damage the environment.
